subject: [PATCH v5 0/9] dax/kmem: atomic whole-device hotplug via sysfs

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges that some reviewer comments remain unaddressed and plans to re-spin the patch in version 6, after the merge window closes.
reviews.raw_body:
Disregard, there are a few unaddressed Sashiko comments, I'm just going
to respin this.  Will wait until after the merge window closes for v6.

The rough shape of things should still hold w/ prior feedback.

~Gregory

2026-06-24.patch_summary: This patch series adds atomic whole-device hotplug control to the dax kmem driver via a sysfs 'state' attribute, allowing for transitions across entire regions of memory to be performed atomically and safely. The current implementation has a race condition between offlining and removing memory blocks, which this patch aims to solve by introducing a new ABI with state names mirroring per-block memoryX/state attributes. The series also includes mm preparation changes to support the new hotplug control, including adding helpers for online type management and atomic offline+remove of multiple ranges. The dax/kmem feature is updated to plumb online_type through its device creation path and extract hotplug/hotremove into helper functions. A selftest is added to regression test the new attribute.
